info.title: Deploying a &ha; &hana; cluster on existing hosts

abstract.para: Deploy highly available (HA) &hana; scale-up system on existing hosts by using &ansible; for automation. With &ansible;, you can achieve a resilient, scalable and enterprise-grade environment suitable for mission-critical workloads with minimal manual intervention. This procedure is intended for system administrators, DevOps engineers, and &sap; Basis consultants who have a working knowledge of &ansible; and &sap; principles.

topic.para: The resulting setup consists of two &hana; database scale-up servers configured in a HA cluster (system replication).

procedure.title: Deployment on existing hosts
procedure.para: Before the deployment, ensure you have two hosts provisioned and running &sles4sap; 16.

step.para: Configure passwordless SSH access from your control node to securely communicate with the existing hosts.
Generate an SSH key if you do not have one. Leave the passphrase empty for passwordless access.
Copy the SSH public key to your existing target hosts.
Add the target hosts to your known_hosts file to prevent manual SSH verification prompts.
Install the sles_sap_automation pattern. The pattern provides &ansible; and the required &sap; roles and playbooks together to prevent dependency issues.
Verify that the pattern is installed with all its components.
step.screen:
&prompt.sudo; zypper info --type pattern sles_sap_automation
Refreshing service 'SUSE_Linux_Enterprise_Server_for_SAP_applications_16.0_x86_64'. Retrieving repository 'SLE-Product-SLES_SAP-16.0' metadata ...[done] Building repository 'SLE-Product-SLES_SAP-16.0' cache ...[done] Loading repository data... Reading installed packages...
Information for pattern sles_sap_automation: -------------------------------------------- Repository : SLE-Product-SLES_SAP-16.0 Name : sles_sap_automation Version : 16.0-160000.5.1 Arch : x86_64 Vendor : SUSE LLC <https://www.suse.com/> Buddy Package : patterns-sap-automation-16.0-160000.5.1.x86_64 Installed : Yes Visible to User : Yes Summary : Automation deployment for SAP systems Description : Ansible playbooks and roles Contents : S | Name | Type | Dependency ---+----------------------------------+---------+----------- i+ | pattern:sles_sap_automation | pattern | Required i | pattern:sles_sap_base_sap_server | pattern | Required i | ansible | package | Required i | ansible-linux-system-roles | package | Required i | ansible-sap-infrastructure | package | Required i | ansible-sap-install | package | Required i | ansible-sap-operations | package | Required i | ansible-sap-playbooks | package | Required i | ansible-trento | package | Required

step.para: Create a dedicated workspace for your deployment.

important.title: Do not modify default playbooks
important.para: Do not modify the default files in /usr/share/ansible/playbooks/ directly. Future package updates will overwrite your changes.

step.para: Create a directory to isolate your configuration files. For example:
Copy the following default variable and inventory files from the sap_hana_ha scenario into your new directory:

listitem.para: optional/ansible_inventory_noninteractive.yml contains inventory definition of existing hosts.

step.para: Update the ansible_extravars.yml variable file.

important.title: Replace variable placeholders
important.para: In the variable files, there is a placeholder ENTER_STRING_VALUE_HERE. You must replace all instances of this placeholder with the actual values from your environment. The following steps include additional information for only certain variables.

step.para: Set the following variable to the topology defined inside ansible_extravars_existing_hosts.yml under sap_vm_provision_existing_hosts_host_specifications_dictionary.
Define the SAP product and installation source. Set the sap_software_product variable to the product defined inside ansible_extravars.yml under sap_software_install_dictionary.

note.title: Software download options
note.para: For a more automated setup, you can install the optional ansible-sap-launchpad package from the &ph;. If this package is present, the playbook can automatically download the software from the &sap; Launchpad. To enable this, you must provide your S-User credentials in sap_id_user and sap_id_user_password variables.

step.para: Enable and define your master password.
step.screen:
# Use a master password for SAP HANA installation (y/n) (String). sap_hana_install_use_master_password: "y"
# The master password for SAP HANA (String). sap_hana_install_master_password: 'YOUR_SECURE_PASSWORD'

step.para: Set the variable for virtual overlay IP addresses. The variable needs to follow a specific convention for a given platform. For example:
step.screen: # oVirt KVM: the VIP must be within the VLAN Subnet range sap_vm_provision_ha_vip_hana_primary: "10.10.10.90"

step.para: Update the ansible_extravars_existing_hosts.yml variable file.
&ansible; automatically detects domain from the /etc/hosts file, but the following variable can override it:
Update the following variable with the path to the SSH key that exists on the control node and is updated in authorized_keys on existing hosts. For example:
Update the ansible_inventory_noninteractive.yml inventory file with the connection details of your hosts. For example:

listitem.para: Simplified SSH arguments without Bastion host.
Simplified SSH arguments without Bastion host.

step.screen:
---
hana_primary: hosts: 01hana: ansible_host: "10.10.10.10" ansible_port: 22 ansible_user: root ansible_ssh_private_key_file: "/root/.ssh/sap_rsa" ansible_ssh_common_args: >- -o ConnectTimeout=180 -o ControlMaster=auto -o ControlPersist=3600s -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null -o ForwardX11=no
hana_secondary: hosts: 02hana: ansible_host: "10.10.10.11" ansible_port: 22 ansible_user: root ansible_ssh_private_key_file: "/root/.ssh/sap_rsa" ansible_ssh_common_args: >- -o ConnectTimeout=180 -o ControlMaster=auto -o ControlPersist=3600s -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null -o ForwardX11=no

note.title: Inventory group names
note.para: Avoid changing the group names (hana_primary, hana_secondary) in the inventory file. These names are used in the deployment playbook. Renaming them would require customizing the playbook to change all host entries.

step.para: Test the &ansible; connection. Verify that &ansible; can successfully connect to your target hosts by using the inventory and SSH keys you configured.
Encrypt the files with &ansible; Vault. Files can be encrypted either with a prompt or a password file. The password file method is easier in larger environments for reusability.
Run the main playbook from your working directory (for example, playbooks/H01/). It starts the automated process of installing the SAP software.
The --vault-password-file option decrypts your sensitive variables during the run.

topic.para: You can now proceed with post-installation tasks, such as:

listitem.para: System validation: Performing health checks and verifying functionality.
Backup and recovery: Implementing backup and recovery strategy.
Monitoring: Integrating the new landscape with your existing monitoring solutions.
